summary: Tested Phases 4-6 (unified Manage hub, CMD+K search, route redirects). 13/14 functional tests passed. One bug: /security-log does NOT redirect to /manage?tab=security as required - it's still defined as a standalone protected route in App.js rendering <SecurityLog /> directly. All other redirects (/admin, /teacher-dashboard, /attendance) work. Manage page loads with 3 admin tabs (Members, Attendance, Security), tab content renders correctly, ?tab= URL param pre-selects tabs, CMD+K opens via header search button + Ctrl+K shortcut, debounced search returns results, Escape closes overlay, selecting a result navigates to /courses/{id}. Avatar dropdown 'Security Log' link goes to /manage?tab=security. Mobile bottom-nav 'Manage' tab works. Console has Radix accessibility warning: CommandDialog is missing DialogTitle (a11y, minor).

ui_bugs.issue: /security-log does NOT redirect to /manage?tab=security. Route is still defined as a standalone <ProtectedRoute requireAdmin><SecurityLog /></ProtectedRoute> at lines 292-299 of /app/frontend/src/App.js. Per the Phase 4 requirement, /security-log must redirect to /manage?tab=security (mirroring how /attendance and /teacher-dashboard already redirect).

Radix console error on every open: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle for the component to be accessible for screen reader users' and 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Wrap a VisuallyHidden DialogTitle inside the CommandDialog (or pass title prop if supported) to fix accessibility warnings.

passed_tests: /manage loads for admin with exactly 3 tabs: Members(admin), Attendance, Security
Manage Members tab renders Admin panel content (analytics/pending/members text present)
Manage Attendance tab renders attendance content
Manage Security tab renders security log content
/attendance -> redirects to /manage?tab=attendance with attendance tab active
CMD+K palette opens via header search icon button (data-testid=header-search-btn)
Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut opens the palette
<2 char input shows 'Type at least 2 characters' helper
Typing 'test' debounces and returns search response (groups or empty msg)
Selecting a course result navigates to /courses/{id}
Desktop avatar dropdown 'Security Log' link href=/manage?tab=security and click navigates correctly
Mobile bottom-nav 'Manage' tab present (data-testid=bottom-nav-manage), click navigates to /manage and renders manage-page
action_items: Replace /security-log route in /app/frontend/src/App.js (lines 292-299) with a Navigate redirect: <Route path='/security-log' element={<Navigate to='/manage?tab=security' replace />} />. Currently it still renders the standalone <SecurityLog /> page bypassing the unified Manage hub.

Fix Radix a11y warning in SearchCommand: add a hidden DialogTitle inside CommandDialog. Easiest fix: import VisuallyHidden from '@radix-ui/react-visually-hidden' (or use a screen-reader-only span) and place <VisuallyHidden><DialogTitle>Search</DialogTitle></VisuallyHidden> inside the CommandDialog before CommandInput. Also add an aria-describedby or DialogDescription.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js inconsistency: /admin, /teacher-dashboard, /attendance correctly redirect to /manage but /security-log was missed - this is the bug.

ManagePage.jsx wraps content in <Layout> while sub-pages (Admin, TeacherDashboard, AttendanceReport, SecurityLog) correctly check the `embedded` prop to skip their own Layout wrapper. Embedding works as designed.

ManagePage.jsx tab state is initialized only once from ?tab= URL param (useState initial). If user navigates from /manage?tab=attendance to /manage?tab=security via browser back/forward, activeTab will not update because useSearchParams change isn't tied to a useEffect. Minor edge case - currently no internal links rely on this, but worth a useEffect that syncs activeTab when searchParams change.

SearchCommand.jsx debounceRef uses React.useRef inside an arrow component; works but mixing var/function and React.useRef hooks is stylistically inconsistent with the rest of the file using `var` and ES5-style function components. Functional, just code-style.
Layout.jsx CMD+K listener correctly preventDefault and uses metaKey||ctrlKey. The native cmdk library inside CommandDialog also handles its own Cmd+K - no conflict observed in test.

success_rate.frontend: 93% (13/14 functional checks passed; 1 routing bug + 1 a11y warning)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Only the /security-log redirect needs verification after main agent fixes App.js. The CommandDialog a11y warning is non-blocking. All 14 listed test cases were exercised on desktop (1920x1080) plus one mobile (390x844) check. Teacher-role manage view (2 tabs: My Group + Attendance) was NOT tested - only admin role. If teacher view is critical, retest with a teacher account.
rca of the issue: Bug: /security-log does not redirect to /manage?tab=security. RCA: In /app/frontend/src/App.js, when Phase 4 redirects were added, the route block for /security-log (lines 292-299) was left as the original standalone protected route rendering <SecurityLog />. The other three legacy routes (/admin, /teacher-dashboard, /attendance) were correctly converted to <Navigate to='/manage...' replace />. Reproduction: login as admin, manually navigate to /security-log -> URL stays /security-log and renders the standalone (non-embedded) SecurityLog page instead of the unified Manage hub. Fix: change the route element to <Navigate to='/manage?tab=security' replace /> (no requireAdmin guard needed because /manage already enforces requireTeacher and the Security tab only renders when isAdmin).
